2605.12422.title: Predicting Disagreement with Human Raters in LLM-as-a-Judge Difficulty Assessment without Using Generation-Time Probability Signals
2605.12422.paper_summary: Automatic generation of educational materials using large language models (LLMs) is becoming increasingly common, but assigning difficulty levels to such materials still requires substantial human effort. LLM-as-a-Judge has therefore attracted attention, yet disagreement with human raters remains a major challenge. We propose a method for predicting which LLM-generated difficulty ratings are likely to disagree with human raters, so that such cases can be sent for re-rating. Unlike prior approaches, our method does not rely on generation-time probability signals, which must be collected during rating generation and are often difficult to compare across LLMs. Instead, exploiting the fact that difficulty is an ordinal scale, we use a separate embedding space, such as ModernBERT, and identify disagreement candidates based on the geometric consistency of the rating set. Experiments on English CEFR-based sentence difficulty assessment with GPT-OSS-120B and Qwen3-235B-A22B showed that the proposed method achieved higher AUC for predicting disagreement with human raters than probability-based baselines.
2605.12422.paper_summary_zh: 自動生成教育材料使用大型語言模型（LLMs）變得越來越普遍，但對這些材料分配難度級別仍然需要大量人力。因此，LLM-as-a-Judge引起了關注，但與人類評估者的分歧仍然是一個主要挑戰。我們提出了一種方法來預測哪些LLM生成的難度評級可能與人類評估者不一致，以便將這些情況發送進行重新評級。與先前的方法不同，我們的方法不依賴於生成時的概率信號，這些信號必須在評級生成過程中收集，並且通常難以在不同LLMs之間進行比較。相反，利用難度是一個有序範疇的事實，我們使用一個單獨的嵌入空間，例如ModernBERT，並根據評級集的幾何一致性來識別不一致的候選項。在使用GPT-OSS-120B和Qwen3-235B-A22B進行的基於英語CEFR的句子難度評估實驗中，所提出的方法在預測與人類評估者不一致方面實現了比基於概率的基準更高的AUC。
2605.12422.author: Yo Ehara et.al.
